title: Drymocallis rupestris
intro: Drymocallis rupestris, also known as mountain avens, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of mountain avens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Mountain avens is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is used in herbal remedies to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
